mentions.note: Private equity tenth-worst S&P performer in February, down 22.3%; posted earnings beat but stock sold off on private credit sector panic; Cramer skeptical concerns overblown but unwilling to be hero.
Private equity with private credit exposure; third-worst S&P performer in February, down 25.2%; sizable earnings miss; stocks traded in lockstep despite different results.
Caller obsessed with sugar-free option; stock hit with no substantive reason; CEO Christine Barone executing well.

After 6-month decline from $126K to low 60Ks, technician Larry Williams signals 85% cycle bias to rally through June; undervalued vs. gold; animal spirits and risk appetite returning; bottom in place.
Networking play benefiting from fiber optic demand for data centers; up 38.5% in February; rejoining S&P 500 after 17 years.
Data center backup power business grew 35% in 2025, forecasting 10–20% growth in 2026; capacity constrained through 2028; truck cycle improving H2 2026.
Started mid-20s, now mid-to-high 50s; strong double-digit EBITDA growth despite tariff costs; hold position near 52-week high, don't add.
Concerns about interest-rate cap threat and AI credit disruption overblown; Fairbank's Discover integration is true value driver; credit fundamentals strong; wild trader—Cramer bought weakness back.
Online marketplace for commercial real estate data; down 27.4% in February; AI can easily replicate proprietary data; strong Q4 but light guidance spooked market.
Data center play up 29.4% in February; huge Q4 beat on AI product sales and margins; strong backlog and bullish full-year outlook; rallied 22% Friday alone.
Medical technology for kidney dialysis; down 24% in 2025 but up 43% in February on terrific quarter; analysts and investors got too negative; stock caught fire.
Outsourced software developer crushed by AI displacement fears; worst S&P performer in February, down 32.4%; light guidance confirmed market's AI displacement worries; down 70% in single session.
Eighth-worst performer in February, down 22.6%, despite stellar earnings beat; sellers worried about streaming competition; now trades 12x earnings—cheap valuation.
Electrical distribution, components, and data center play; incredible stock up since Feb 5; Cramer plans separate deep-dive coverage.
Fiber optic specialist up 45.7% in February, 96% gain in trust since September; $6B Meta data center deal and strong guidance catalyzed rally; Cramer's aha moment in Kentucky.
Backup generator and home solar play up 34% in February; weak Q4 from light hurricane season but strong 2026 outlook; data center and severe winter weather driving growth.
Down ~10% Friday pre-Iran strike; Cramer and Jeff Marks both flagged 15x earnings as absurdly cheap; Lloyd Blankfein's 'Streetwise' explains valuation; way too cheap.
Sixth-worst S&P performer in February, down 24%, on miserable quarter (soft options/crypto trading); now hostage to crypto and predictions betting; Cramer prefers SoFi for young people fintech.
Aerospace bull market remains fully in place; up 26.2% in February; up 800% over past 5 years; 'has no quit in it'; aerospace and defense complex looks terrific.

Contract research organization (CRO) ninth-worst in February, down 22.3%, on weaker full-year forecast despite solid beat; caught in AI displacement panic unfairly; RFK Jr.'s FDA skepticism on new drugs a headwind.
IT consulting firm down 25% in February on AI displacement fears; customers rely on Gartner expertise that may become less valuable; light full-year forecast spooked market.
Electronic design automation and test/measurement for AI data centers; up 42% in February; blowout quarter with strong guidance.
Private equity firm seventh-worst S&P performer in February, down 23.3%; small earnings miss; stocks trading in lockstep on private credit fears despite divergent results.
Materials supplier for advanced chip production; DuPont spin-off; up 31.8% in February; blowout quarter and one of Cramer's favorite new stocks; breakup successful.
Defense supplier with strong order backlog; defense department needs weapons; stock winner even at current elevated levels.
Rare earth producer; caller owned since 2020; long-term growth potential given president's commitment to domestic critical minerals; not just spec play.
Cramer 'very worried' about CAPEX spending on AI infrastructure; considering selling some or all despite trust owning it—huge change for him.
Walking away from WB bid good for balance sheet; stock moved 10% but Cramer would buy at current levels.
Cramer regrets not owning; much better drug bet than peers with exception of Eli Lilly; should have pulled trigger at JP Morgan conference.
Semiconductor testing and measurement play; up 32.8% in February; semiconductor complex performing well despite broader tech weakness.
Best S&P performer in February, up 50.5%, on oil spike and data center optionality; energy tailwind may reverse if peace breaks out with Iran.

Enterprise software down 23.8% in February; CEO Eschenbach departed, replaced by co-founder Aneel Bhusri; lowered full-year guidance 3 months after prior forecast; now 13x earnings but cohort too hated for bottom-fishing.
